cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.22)

#
# This file is generated only once,
# and is not re-generated if converter is called multiple times.
#
# User is free to modify the file as much as necessary
#

# Setup compiler settings
set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD 11)
set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
set(CMAKE_C_EXTENSIONS ON)


# Define the build type
if(NOT CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE)
    set(CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE "Debug")
endif()

# Generate version.h at configure time (initial)
find_package(Git QUIET)

if(GIT_FOUND)
    execute_process(
        COMMAND ${GIT_EXECUTABLE} describe --tags --dirty --always
        WORKING_DIRECTORY ${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}
        OUTPUT_VARIABLE FW_GIT_DESCRIBE
        OUTPUT_STRIP_TRAILING_WHITESPACE
        RESULT_VARIABLE GIT_DESCRIBE_RESULT
    )
    execute_process(
        COMMAND ${GIT_EXECUTABLE} rev-parse --short HEAD
        WORKING_DIRECTORY ${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}
        OUTPUT_VARIABLE FW_GIT_SHA
        OUTPUT_STRIP_TRAILING_WHITESPACE
        RESULT_VARIABLE GIT_SHA_RESULT
    )
    if(NOT GIT_DESCRIBE_RESULT EQUAL 0)
        set(FW_GIT_DESCRIBE "unknown")
    endif()
    if(NOT GIT_SHA_RESULT EQUAL 0)
        set(FW_GIT_SHA "unknown")
    endif()
else()
    set(FW_GIT_DESCRIBE "unknown")
    set(FW_GIT_SHA "unknown")
endif()

string(TIMESTAMP FW_BUILD_TIME_UTC "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S UTC" UTC)

message(STATUS "FW version   : ${FW_GIT_DESCRIBE}")
message(STATUS "FW git sha   : ${FW_GIT_SHA}")
message(STATUS "FW build time: ${FW_BUILD_TIME_UTC}")

configure_file(
    ${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/version.h.in
    ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/generated/version.h
    @ONLY
)

# Set the project name
set(CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME lifu-console-bl)

# Enable compile command to ease indexing with e.g. clangd
set(CMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS TRUE)

# Core project settings
project(${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME})
message("Build type: " ${CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE})

# Enable CMake support for ASM and C languages
enable_language(C ASM)

# Create an executable object type
add_executable(${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME})

# Add STM32CubeMX generated sources
add_subdirectory(cmake/stm32cubemx)

# Link directories setup
target_link_directories(${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME} PRIVATE
    # Linker/ contains mapping_sbsfu.ld / mapping_fwimg.ld pulled in by the
    # top-level script's INCLUDE directives
    "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/Linker"
)

# Collect SBSFU App/Target sources
file(GLOB SBSFU_APP_SOURCES    SBSFU/App/Src/*.c)
file(GLOB SBSFU_TARGET_SOURCES SBSFU/Target/Src/*.c)

# Add sources to executable
target_sources(${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME} PRIVATE
    # SBSFU bootloader core
    ${SBSFU_APP_SOURCES}
    ${SBSFU_TARGET_SOURCES}
    # SVC handler + application launch stubs (Thumb-1 / Cortex-M0)
    SBSFU/App/Src/sfu_se_mpu.s

    # Secure Engine core (single-image build: linked into the bootloader ELF;
    # the call gate / key block / SE startup are pinned by the linker script)
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core/se_callgate.c
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core/se_exception.c
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core/se_fwimg.c
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core/se_startup.c
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core/se_user_application.c
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core/se_utils.c
    # SE Interface shims (must land in the SE_IF flash region: placed by
    # object name in STM32F072xx_FLASH.ld — this is why LTO stays OFF)
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core/se_interface_bootloader.c
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core/se_interface_application.c
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core/se_interface_common.c

    # SECoreBin sources (SE crypto backend + SE low level + SE data init)
    SECoreBin/Src/se_crypto_bootloader.c
    SECoreBin/Src/se_low_level.c
    SECoreBin/Src/data_init.c
    # Product keys (generated by py-tools/generate_keys.py — never commit keys)
    SECoreBin/Startup/se_key.s
)

# The se_interface_*.c marshalling shims must fit the SE_IF flash region and,
# more importantly, must be compact at every build type (ST ships them -Os).
set_source_files_properties(
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core/se_interface_bootloader.c
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core/se_interface_application.c
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core/se_interface_common.c
    PROPERTIES COMPILE_OPTIONS "-Os"
)

# Add include paths
target_include_directories(${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME} PRIVATE
    ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/generated
    # Core/Inc first so the project's stm32f0xx_hal_conf.h takes precedence
    Core/Inc
    # SBSFU / SE includes
    SECoreBin/Inc
    SBSFU/App/Inc
    SBSFU/Target/Inc
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Interface
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Key
    # ST X-CUBE-CRYPTOLIB V5 (CMOX) headers. include/hash and include/mac are
    # required because several internal headers use unprefixed #includes.
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Cryptographic/include
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Cryptographic/include/hash
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Cryptographic/include/mac
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Cryptographic/include/ecc
    # Linker/ is not needed for C includes (mapping_export.h lives in
    # SBSFU/App/Inc) but harmless if mapping files move later
    Linker
)

# Add project symbols (macros)
target_compile_definitions(${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME} PRIVATE
    # Enables SFU_DEBUG_MODE traces on USART3 (see app_sfu.h); flip off if the
    # Release image ever needs the extra flash
    DEBUG_ENABLED
)

# Remove wrong libob.a library dependency when using cpp files
list(REMOVE_ITEM CMAKE_C_IMPLICIT_LINK_LIBRARIES ob)

# Add linked libraries
target_link_libraries(${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME}
    stm32cubemx

    # ST X-CUBE-CRYPTOLIB V5 (CMOX), Cortex-M0/M0+ prebuilt archive: provides
    # SHA-256 + ECDSA P-256 verify for the Secure Engine (se_crypto_bootloader.c).
    "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/Middlewares/ST/STM32_Cryptographic/lib/libSTM32Cryptographic_CM0_CM0PLUS.a"
)

# NOTE: LTO is intentionally NOT enabled. The linker script places the SE
# interface code and SE statics by OBJECT FILE NAME (se_interface_*, se_*);
# -flto merges objects and silently breaks that placement, which would make
# the SE call gate reset the MCU at the first service call.

# Generate hex and bin files after build
add_custom_command(TARGET ${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME} POST_BUILD
    COMMAND ${CMAKE_OBJCOPY} -O ihex $<TARGET_FILE:${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME}> ${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME}.hex
    COMMAND ${CMAKE_OBJCOPY} -O binary $<TARGET_FILE:${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME}> ${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME}.bin
    COMMENT "Generating .hex and .bin files"
)
